rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by adding behavior only within a valid owner scope and leaving existing structural elements unchanged.
Keep type-level contracts with visible component features only; keep implementation-level bindings in implementation scope.
Do not move or alter subcomponents, connections, features, or property associations when inserting annex content.
generic_rules: Match inserted content to the owner kind: component type for interface-visible relations, implementation for implementation-scoped bindings.
Avoid cross-scope references that are not visible from the chosen owner; implementation-only paths must not be treated as type-scope names.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Annex blocks must be attached to a valid AADL component or implementation owner.
Implementation-scoped content should appear in the implementation body without disturbing existing declarations and ordering constraints.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-scoped bindings under a component type owner.
Do not rewrite architecture structure as part of annex insertion.
Do not reference AADL property names or unit tokens as if they were in expression scope.
irrelevant_topics: Validator-specific type-repair tactics unless diagnostics are provided
Requirement-level threshold semantics beyond deciding proper owner/scope
